description_long: This is a double-ended open wrench made of metal, exhibiting a weathered and rusty surface throughout. Both ends feature U-shaped, fixed jaws of different sizes, designed for gripping and turning nuts or bolts. The body is flat and elongated, with a slight taper toward each end, and the entire surface is covered in a reddish-brown patina indicative of significant rust and oxidation. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, and the handle is smooth without any grip pattern. The tool appears utilitarian and well-used, likely found in garages, workshops, or urban maintenance settings.
description: A rusted, double-ended open wrench with fixed jaws and a flat, elongated metal body. Both ends show U-shaped openings, and the surface is heavily oxidized.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wrench, both open jaws clearly visible, and the rusted surface texture.
description_view_1: Edge-on view showing the thin, flat profile of the wrench and the narrowness of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with both open jaws and the rusted surface visible.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again showing the thin profile and flatness of the wrench.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Extensive rust and oxidation cover the entire surface, with a uniform reddish-brown patina and no pristine areas.
